sections.label: Section 539
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν , ὦναξ · μὴ τοσόνδ ’ ἔλθοι κακόν .
sections.translation: Never , O prince ! Heaven forefend such dire disgrace !

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: λυπουμένοις ὀχληρός , εἰ μόλοι , ξένος .
sections.translation: A guest is a burden to sorrowing friends , if come he should .

sections.label: Section 541
sections.original: τεθνᾶσιν οἱ θανόντες · ἀλλ ’ ἴθ ’ ἐς δόμους .
sections.translation: The dead are dead . Come in .

sections.label: Section 542
sections.original: αἰσχρὸν παρὰ κλαίουσι θοινᾶσθαι ξένους .
sections.translation: To feast in a friend ’ s house of sorrow is shameful .

sections.label: Section 543
sections.original: χωρὶς ξενῶνές εἰσιν οἷ σ ’ ἐσάξομεν .
sections.translation: The guest chambers lie apart , whereto we will conduct thee .

sections.label: Section 544
sections.original: μέθες με , καί σοι μυρίαν ἕξω χάριν .
sections.translation: Let me go ; ten thousandfold shall be my thanks to thee .

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν ἄλλου σ ’ ἀνδρὸς ἑστίαν μολεῖν .
sections.translation: Thou must not go to any other hearth . ( To a Servant .) Go before , open the guest-rooms that face not these chambers , and bid my stewards see there is plenty of food ; then shut the doors that lead into the courtyard ; for ’ tis not seemly that guests when at their meat

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: κλύειν στεναγμῶν οὐδὲ λυπεῖσθαι ξένους .
sections.translation: should hear the voice of weeping or be made sad . [ Exit HERACLES .

sections.label: Section 551
sections.original: τί δρᾷς ; τοιαύτης συμφορᾶς προκειμένης ,
sections.translation: What doest thou ? With such calamity before thee , hast thou the heart , Admetus , to welcome visitors ? What means this folly ?

sections.label: Section 553
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ εἰ δόμων σφε καὶ πόλεως ἀπήλασα
sections.translation: Well , and if I had driven him from my house and city when he came to be my guest , wouldst thou have praised me more ?

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ , ἐπεί μοι συμφορὰ μὲν οὐδὲν ἂν
sections.translation: No indeed ! for my calamity would have been no whit less , while I should have been more churlish . And this would have been another woe to add to mine , that my house should be called no friend to guests . Yea , and I find him myself the best of hosts

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: ὅταν ποτ ’ Ἄργους διψίαν ἔλθω χθόνα .
sections.translation: whene ’ er to Argos ’ thirsty land I come .

sections.label: Section 561
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἔκρυπτες τὸν παρόντα δαίμονα ,
sections.translation: Why then didst thou conceal thy present misfortune , if , as thy own lips declare , it was a friend that came ?

sections.label: Section 563
sections.original: οὐκ ἄν ποτ ’ ἠθέλησεν εἰσελθεῖν δόμους ,
sections.translation: He would never have entered my house , had he known aught of my distress .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: καὶ τῷ μέν , οἶμαι , δρῶν τάδ ’ οὐ φρονεῖν δοκῶ ,
sections.translation: Maybe there are who think me but a fool for acting thus , and these will blame me ; but my halls have never learnt to drive away or treat with scorn my guests .

sections.label: Section 1.570
sections.original: σέ τοι καὶ ὁ Πύθιος εὐλύρας Ἀπόλλων
sections.translation: In thee it was that Pythian Apollo , the sweet harper , deigned to make his home , and in thy halls was content to lead a shepherd ’ s life ,

sections.label: Section 1.575
sections.original: δοχμιᾶν διὰ κλιτύων
sections.translation: piping o ’ er the sloping downs shepherd ’ s madrigals to thy flocks .

sections.label: Section 1.578
sections.original: σὺν δ ’ ἐποιμαίνοντο χαρᾷ μελέων βαλιαί τε λύγκες ,
sections.translation: And spotted lynxes couched amid his sheep in joy to hear his melody ,

sections.label: Section 1.580
sections.original: ἔβα δὲ λιποῦσ ’ Ὄθρυος νάπαν λεόντων
sections.translation: and the lions ’ tawny troop left the glen of Othrys and came ; came too the dappled fawn on nimble foot from beyond the crested pines and frisked about thy lyre , O Phoebus ,

sections.label: Section 1.585
sections.original: νεβρὸς ὑψικόμων πέραν
sections.translation: for very joy at thy gladsome minstrelsy .

sections.label: Section 2.588
sections.original: τοιγὰρ πολυμηλοτάταν
sections.translation: And so it is thy lord inhabits a home rich in countless flocks

sections.label: Section 2.590
sections.original: Βοιβίαν λίμναν . ἀρότοις δὲ γυᾶν
sections.translation: by Boebe ’ s lovely mere , bounding his tilled corn-land and his level pastures with the clime of the Molossi near the sun ’ s dark stable ,

sections.label: Section 2.595
sections.original: πόντιον δ ’ Αἰγαίων ’ ἐπ ’ ἀκτὰν
sections.translation: and holding sway as far as the harbourless strand of the Aegean ’ neath Pelion ’ s shadow .

sections.label: Section 2.597
sections.original: καὶ νῦν δόμον ἀμπετάσας
sections.translation: Now too hath he opened wide his house and welcomed a guest although his eye is wet with tears in mourning for his wife so dear

sections.label: Section 2.600
sections.original: δώμασιν ἀρτιθανῆ · τὸ γὰρ εὐγενὲς
sections.translation: but lately dead within his halls ; yea , for noble birth to noble feeling is inclined . [*] And in the good completest wisdom dwells ; and at my heart sits the bold belief

sections.label: Section 2.605
sections.original: θεοσεβῆ φῶτα κεδνὰ πράξειν .
sections.translation: that heaven ’ s servant will be .

sections.label: Section 606
sections.original: ἀνδρῶν Φεραίων εὐμενὴς παρουσία ,
sections.translation: Men of Pherae , kindly gathered here , lo ! even now my servants are bearing the corpse with all its trappings shoulder-high to the funeral pyre for burial ; do ye , as custom bids ,

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: προσείπατ ’ ἐξιοῦσαν ὑστάτην ὁδόν .
sections.translation: salute the dead on her last journey starting .

sections.label: Section 611
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὁρῶ σὸν πατέρα γηραιῷ ποδὶ
sections.translation: Look ! I see thy father advancing with aged step , and servants too bearing in their arms adornment for thy wife , offerings for the dead .

sections.label: Section 614
sections.original: ἥκω κακοῖσι σοῖσι συγκάμνων , τέκνον ·
sections.translation: My son , I come to share thy sorrow ,

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: ἐσθλῆς γάρ , οὐδεὶς ἀντερεῖ , καὶ σώφρονος
sections.translation: for thou hast lost a noble , peerless wife ; that no man will deny . Yet must thou needs bear this blow , hard though it be . Accept this garniture , and let it go beneath the earth , for rightly is her body honoured ,

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: ἥτις γε τῆς σῆς προύθανε ψυχῆς , τέκνον ,
sections.translation: since she died to save thy life , my son , and gave me back my child , suffering me not to lose thee and pine away in an old age of sorrow . Thus by the generous deed she dared , hath she made her life a noble example for all her sex .

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: ὦ τόνδε μὲν σώσασʼ , ἀναστήσασα δὲ
sections.translation: Farewell to thee , who hast saved this son of mine and raised me up when falling ; be thine a happy lot even in Hades ’ halls ! Such marriages I declare are gain to man , else to wed is not worth while .

sections.label: Section 629
sections.original: οὔτ ’ ἦλθες ἐς τόνδ ’ ἐξ ἐμοῦ κληθεὶς τάφον ,
sections.translation: Thou hast come uncalled by me to this burial ,

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: οὔτ ’ ἐν φίλοισι σὴν παρουσίαν λέγω .
sections.translation: nor do I count thy presence as a friendly act . Never shall she be clad in any garniture of thine , nor [*] in her burial will she need aught of thine . Thou shouldst have shewn thy sympathy at the time my doom was sealed . But thou didst stand aloof and let another die ,

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: νέῳ γέρων ὤν , τόνδ ’ ἀποιμώξῃ νεκρόν ;
sections.translation: though thou wert old , the victim young ; shalt thou then mourn the dead ? Methinks thou wert no real sire of mine nor was she my true mother who calls herself and is called so , but [*] I was sprung of slave ’ s blood and privily substituted at thy wife ’ s breast .

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: ἔδειξας εἰς ἔλεγχον ἐξελθὼν ὃς εἶ ,
sections.translation:
Brought to the test thou hast shewn thy nature ; I cannot think I am thy child by birth .
By heaven , thou art the very pattern of cowards , who at thy age , on the borderland of life , wouldst not , nay ! couldst not find the heart to die

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: τοῦ σοῦ πρὸ παιδός , ἀλλὰ τήνδ ’ εἰάσατε
sections.translation: for thy own son ; but ye , my parents , left to this stranger , whom I henceforth shall justly hold e ’ en as mother and as father too , and none but her . And yet ’ twas a noble exploit to achieve , to die to save thy son , and in any case the

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: πάντως ὁ λοιπὸς ἦν βιώσιμος χρόνος .
sections.translation: remnant of thy time to live was but short ; and [*] I and she would have lived the days that were to be , nor had I lost my wife and mourned my evil fate . Moreover thou hast had all treatment that a happy man should have ; in princely pomp thy youth was spent ,

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: παῖς δ ’ ἦν ἐγώ σοι τῶνδε διάδοχος δόμων ,
sections.translation: thou hadst a son , myself , to be the heir of this thy home , so thou hadst no fear of dying childless and leaving thy house desolate , for strangers to pillage . Nor yet canst thou say I did dishonour thy old age and give thee up to die , seeing I have ever been

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: πρὸς σ ’ ἦ μάλιστα · κἀντὶ τῶνδέ μοι χάριν
sections.translation: to thee most dutiful , and for this thou , my sire , and she , my mother , have made me this return . Go then , get other sons to tend thy closing years , prepare thy body for the grave , and lay out thy corpse .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: οὐ γάρ σ ’ ἔγωγε τῇδ ’ ἐμῇ θάψω χερί ·
sections.translation: For I will never bury thee with hand of mine ; for I am dead for all thou didst for me ; but if I found a saviour in another and still live , his son I say I am , and his fond nurse in old age will be . ’ Tis vain , I see , the old man ’ s prayer for death ,

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: γῆρας ψέγοντες καὶ μακρὸν χρόνον βίου ·
sections.translation: his plaints at age and life ’ s long weariness . For if death do but draw near , not one doth wish to die ; old age no more they count so burdensome .

sections.label: Section 674
sections.original: ὦ παῖ , πατρὸς δὲ μὴ παροξύνῃς φρένας .
sections.translation: Peace ! enough the present sorrow , O my son ; goad not thy father ’ s soul to fury .

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: ὦ παῖ , τίν ’ αὐχεῖς , πότερα Λυδὸν ἢ Φρύγα
sections.translation: Child , whom think ’ st thou art reviling ? some Lydian or Phrygian bought with thy money ? Art not aware I am a freebom Thessalian , son of a Thessalian sire ? Thou art too insolent ; yet from hence thou shalt not go as thou earnest ,

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: ῥίπτων ἐς ἡμᾶς οὐ βαλὼν οὕτως ἄπει .
sections.translation: after shooting out thy braggart tongue at me . To rule my house I begat and bred thee up ; I own no debt of dying in thy stead ; this is not the law that I received from my ancestors that fathers should die for children , nor is it a custom in Hellas .

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: σαυτῷ γὰρ εἴτε δυστυχὴς εἴτ ’ εὐτυχὴς
sections.translation: For weal or woe , thy life must be thine own ; whate ’ er was due from me to thee , thou hast . Dominion wide is thine , and acres broad I will leave to thee , for from my father did I inherit them . How , pray , have I wronged thee ? of what am I robbing thee ?

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: μὴ θνῇσχ ’ ὑπὲρ τοῦδ ’ ἀνδρός , οὐδ ’ ἐγὼ πρὸ σοῦ .
sections.translation: Die not thou for me , nor I for thee . Thy joy is in the light ; think ’ st thou thy sire ’ s is not ? By Heaven ! ’ tis a weary while , I trow , that time beneath the earth , and life , though short , is sweet . Thou at least didst struggle hard to ’ scape thy death ,

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: καὶ ζῇς παρελθὼν τὴν πεπρωμένην τύχην ,
sections.translation: lost to shame , and by her death dost live beyond thy destined term . Dost thou then speak of cowardice in me , thou craven heart ! no match for thy wife , who hath died for thee , her fine young lord ? A clever scheme hast thou devised to stave off death for ever ,

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: εἰ τὴν παροῦσαν κατθανεῖν πείσεις ἀεὶ
sections.translation: if thou canst persuade each new wife to die instead of thee ; and dost thou then taunt thy friends , who will not do the like , coward as thou art thyself ? Hold thy peace ; reflect , if thou dost love thy life so well , this love by all is shared ; yet if thou wilt speak ill of me ,

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: ἐρεῖς , ἀκούσῃ πολλὰ κοὐ ψευδῆ κακά .
sections.translation: thyself shalt hear a full and truthful list of thy own crimes .

sections.label: Section 706
sections.original: πλείω λέλεκται νῦν τε καὶ τὰ πρὶν κακά ·
sections.translation: Too long that list both now and heretofore ; cease , father , to revile thy son .

sections.label: Section 708
sections.original: λέγʼ , ὡς ἐμοῦ λέξαντος · εἰ δ ’ ἀλγεῖς κλύων
sections.translation: Say on , for I have said my say ; but if it vexes thee to hear the truth , thou shouldst not have sinned against me .

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: σοῦ δ ’ ἂν προθνῄσκων μᾶλλον ἐξημάρτανον .
sections.translation: My sin had been the deeper , had I died for thee .

sections.label: Section 711
sections.original: ταὐτὸν γὰρ ἡβῶντ ’ ἄνδρα καὶ πρέσβυν θανεῖν ;
sections.translation: What ! is it all one for young or old to die ?

sections.label: Section 712
sections.original: ψυχῇ μιᾷ ζῆν , οὐ δυοῖν , ὀφείλομεν .
sections.translation: To live one life , not twain , is all our due .

sections.label: Section 713
sections.original: καὶ μὴν Διός γε μείζονα ζῴης χρόνον .
sections.translation: Outlive then Zeus himself !

sections.label: Section 714
sections.original: ἀρᾷ γονεῦσιν οὐδὲν ἔκδικον παθών ;
sections.translation: Dost curse thy parents , though unharmed by them ?

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: μακροῦ βίου γὰρ ᾐσθόμην ἐρῶντά σε .
sections.translation: Yea , for I see thy heart is set on length of days .

sections.label: Section 716
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ οὐ σὺ νεκρὸν ἀντὶ σοῦ τόνδ ’ ἐκφέρεις ;
sections.translation: Is it not to save thyself thou art carrying to the tomb this corpse ?
